rulings.Case Title: STEVEN FONTANA SR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Appoint Debra Fontana As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Pltf And For Leave To File 2nd Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff's motion to appoint Debra Fontana as successor-in-interest to, and substitute successor-in-interest for, deceased Plaintiff and for leave to file a second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. =(220/JJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT NELSON et al VS. AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, March 30, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs' demurrer to Defendant Hill Brothers Chemical Company's first, ninth, fourteenth, fifteenth, and sixteenth affirmative defenses is sustained without leave to amend because the defendant's allegations are not affirmative defenses. Plaintiff's demurrer to Defendant's third affirmative defense is sustained without leave to amend because laches is an equitable defense unavailable in legal actions. Plaintiff's demurrer to Defendant's fourth, fifth, sixth, eighth, tenth, twelfth and thirteenth affirmative defenses is sustained with 10 days leave to amend because the Defendant has failed to plead the facts underlying these defenses. Plaintiff's demurrer to Defendant's seventh and eleventh affirmative defenses is overruled because the complaint and answer together allege facts stating these defenses. =(220/JJM)
